Making TV and Internet Safe for Children
nytimes_articles.abstract:
LIFE'S unpleasantnesses have a nasty way of appearing unexpectedly on television screens or computer monitors. And children have a way of being there  when they do. 

     While parents may feel powerless to shield children from offensive words and  images on television, in video and computer games and on line, there are many ways to limit children's access to the burgeoning electronic world. Recent Federal laws and new technology are making it easier.

Wild Pitch
nytimes_articles.abstract:
Even at a time when major league sports have become a cartoon of financial excess, the proposed new home for the Yankees is breathtaking in its audacity. Excluding land value, a multipurpose mausoleum on Manhattan's West Side would cost a billion dollars. 

     The debate has centered on two questions: Are sports facilities economically  worthwhile? And should the stadium be single-purpose or multipurpose? A more fundamental question is why cities provide large subsidies to teams in the first place.

PRO BASKETBALL;Wilkins Is a Stranger In a Strange Game
nytimes_articles.abstract:
It's not easy to explain, and back home they would never believe it, but the  game Dominique Wilkins plays Thursday night for a Greek club might represent his highest professional achievement. And never mind that he is the eighth-highest scorer in National Basketball Association history. 

     His club, Panathinaikos of Athens, could become the first Greek team to win the European championship when it meets FC Barcelona in the final here. Although it would be Wilkins's first title since high school, the event would be no match, say, for his seventh-game, fourth-quarter duel with the Atlanta Hawks against Larry Bird and the Celtics in the 1988 N.B.A. playoffs.

Small Packages Make Good Things Happen
nytimes_articles.abstract:
TWO-THIRDS of all New Yorkers live in tight quarters -- four rooms or fewer -- and many more may soon feel the squeeze: small apartments, according to the Real Estate Board of New York, a trade group, are being purchased faster now than in the last three years. 

     Still, designers know that a compact home, once you stop fighting the boundaries, can be about comfort, intimacy, even a measure of glamour. A little  room with red walls, for example, can feel like a jewel. Painted white, with no  other decorative treatment, it may simply look . . . well, small.

The Korea Divide
nytimes_articles.abstract:
North Korea's dangerous posturing in the demilitarized zone has dramatized the need to replace the 1953 armistice, which has kept a shaky peace between the two Koreas, with a new system that reflects post-cold-war realignments. 

     When the pact was signed, the North was a military base for the Soviet Union  and China. Now Russia and China have much closer economic and political ties with South Korea than with the North, and the North has urgently sought to normalize relations with the United States.

wiki_events.headline: Two women and four children are killed after Israeli helicopter fired rockets at an ambulance in Mansouri, Lebanon.

pages.title: Mansouri attack
pages.abstract: The Mansouri attack occurred on 13 April 1996, when an Israel Defence Forces helicopter attacked an ambulance in Mansouri, a village in Southern Lebanon, killing two women and four children.

pages.title: Al-Mansouri
pages.abstract: Al-Mansouri is a municipality in the Tyre District in South Lebanon.
